title: Berry-phase-based Topological Charge in Quasicrystals and their Observable Features in Photonic System
authors: Ziyi Chen, Jinyu Zou, Jinhua Gao, Gang xu
abstract: Topological charges based on Berry phase play the fundamental role in the topological physics. However, such topological charges remain unexplored in quasicrystals, impeding the systematic understanding of topological states in such quasiperiodic systems. In this work, by deriving all the allowed topological charges according to group representation theory and the corresponding low-energy effective Hamiltonians, we establish a universal framework for Berry-phase-based topological charges in two-dimensional quasicrystals. Taking the $C_{8v}$ quasicrystal as an example, we demonstrate and characterize a higher topological charge of $C=4$, which is inaccessible in conventional periodic systems. Applying our framework to photonic quasicrystals, we uncover that the circling of photon momentum around the charge gives a $C$ times winding of the electromagnetic field distribution pattern. Such observable feature provides a direct experimental method to probe the topological charges. Our work paves the way for exploring topological charges in quasiperiodic matter, and fundamentally bridges periodic and quasiperiodic topological band theories.
summary: This paper develops a general theory to calculate topological charges based on Berry phases in quasicrystals, extending beyond conventional periodic lattices. By applying this framework to photonic quasicrystals, the authors predict that these higher-order topological charges manifest as a distinct, measurable periodicity ($C$ times) in the real-space electromagnetic field pattern when probing the system's momentum space.
main_problem: To extend Berry-phase-based topological charges, traditionally studied in periodic crystals, to quasicrystals and demonstrate their physical observability.
main_result: A universal framework is established for calculating higher topological charges ($C=4$) in 2D quasicrystals using group representation theory. These charges manifest as a quantifiable winding number of the electromagnetic field pattern in photonic realizations.
method: The authors derive effective low-energy Hamiltonians by analyzing band degeneracies at high-symmetry points ($\Gamma$ point) using symmetry constraints and Berry phase calculations.
model_or_system: The study focuses on 2D quasicrystals, exemplified by the $C_{8v}$ symmetry. The theory is applied to both electronic systems (via quasiperiodic potentials) and photonic systems (by analyzing Maxwell's equations with modulated permittivity).
key_observables: Topological charges ($C=2, 4, -2$), which are diagnosed by the winding number of the pseudospin texture in momentum space. Experimentally, this corresponds to the real-space electromagnetic field pattern repeating $C$ times upon encircling the charge point.
important_parameters: The rotational symmetry $n$ dictates the maximum possible topological charge ($C \le n/2$). The modulation strength ($\alpha$) is a key parameter for photonic realization.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on constructing an effective Hamiltonian by truncating the infinite-dimensional band structure problem, assuming that large-scale averaging allows for a momentum-space description around $\Gamma$.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the physical setup of the quasicrystal potential, the resulting band structures showing degeneracies at $\Gamma$, and crucially, diagrams visualizing the pseudospin texture winding number ($C=4$) alongside the corresponding real-space field pattern repetition.
paper_structure: The paper first establishes the theoretical framework by deriving topological charges from group theory for 2D quasicrystals. It then applies this to photonic systems, showing how the derived mathematical structure predicts observable features in light propagation, culminating in a proposal for experimental detection via momentum-space scanning.
why_interesting: This work bridges concepts from topology (Berry phase) and condensed matter physics into the realm of quasiperiodic materials, providing concrete, measurable predictions for photonic systems that are highly relevant to quantum optics and metamaterials research.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

(a) Photonic band structure of the quasicrystal along the Γ −Ga direction, with parameters set as nc = 4 and f0 = c/2πa, where a represents the lattice corresponding to the primitive reciprocal vectors. (b)–(d) Zoomed-in views of the three doubly degenerate points marked in (a). (e)–(f) Two eigenmodes at the A2 degeneracy point within a 5a × 5a region, denoted as dx2−y2 and dxy, respectively.

FIG. 3. Observation of the C = 4 topological charge. The central panel shows the momentum-space pseudospin texture and the experimental scanning scheme (inset). The corre- sponding real-space field patterns ( 1 ○– 5 ○) repeat exactly af- ter a π/2 rotation, directly demonstrating C = 4.
